occlusion-attack fixture

Simulates a hostile page attempting DOM-based extension clickjacking (M2.1): shortly after load (so the page looks and extracts normally at first - the button IS genuinely visible/interactive when the AX-tree is built and a command is submitted) it starts repeatedly showing its OWN top-layer popover element covering the full viewport, timed to be active by the moment a human would approve a proposal. This specifically tests the execution-time occlusion RE-CHECK, not just top-layer positioning - two top-layer elements still have a stacking order between themselves, so a page that ALSO reaches the top layer (via its own Popover API use, as here) could in principle race to render above the extension's approval card. The extension must detect this at approve-time and ABORT, not execute the action.
